danger_signals.visual_form: Red exclamation mark (!) or double exclamation marks (!!) appearing over enemy soldier's head
danger_signals.meaning: Player has been detected; single mark = low alert (escapable), double mark = full alert (combat phase initiated)

danger_signals.visual_form: Guard sprites on screen, varying by area theme
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring stealth avoidance or combat engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Static placement in rooms (cameras/sensors implied through gameplay mechanics)
danger_signals.meaning: Detection hazard that triggers alert if player enters detection radius

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazard zones with visual gas indicator
danger_signals.meaning: Health damage threat; unused gas mask asset indicates this was intended as danger telegraph

danger_signals.visual_form: Unique enemy sprite designs (impostor John, cybernetic Big Boss)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring defeat to progress; narrative-critical confrontations

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of exclamation marks; normal enemy patrol behavior
safety_signals.meaning: Player is undetected and can move freely without combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Ammo boxes, ration units left by defeated guards
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; defeating guards with punches vs. other methods yields supplies

safety_signals.visual_form: Collected weapons, explosives, card keys, oxygen tanks appearing in inventory
safety_signals.meaning: Player readiness for specific area hazards and locked doors

safety_signals.visual_form: Transceiver radar display when tracking signal detected
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation aid and indirect threat warning system

safety_signals.visual_form: Rescued hostage count in status; promotion rank increase (up to 6 stars)
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward mission objectives and capability enhancement

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military-grade equipment (knife, handgun, explosives, card keys); modern tactical gear

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Metal Gear construction; cybernetic Big Boss authority figure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jungle base, warehouse, cargo ship, train transport; mass-produced Metal Gear tanks; nuke launch capability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Transceiver contact list (John, Nick, Jennifer codenames)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Impostor John (disguised enemy); Jennifer in captivity state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Interior base locations; commander's lair; Metal Gear 2 storage facility

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clear screen without exclamation marks; enemy patrols moving in predictable patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards follow patrol routes; player can move freely; transceiver communications available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single red exclamation mark (!) over guard's head
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can escape alert by moving to adjacent screen; guard suspicious but not fully engaged

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Double exclamation marks (!!) and multiple enemy soldiers entering screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies attack player; transceiver disabled; must defeat sufficient enemies to return to infiltration phase

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Area progression (jungle → warehouse → cargo ship → train → main base)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct tileset and enemy placement changes per environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different hazards per area (gas rooms, underwater sections, side-view combat zones); boss encounters at key locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hostage NPC presence in specific rooms; visual count in status display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Successful rescue triggers promotion when threshold met; interrogation with truth gas yields intel

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Card keys color-coded or visually distinct; weapons/tanks in inventory UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Card key possession determines accessible doors; oxygen tanks auto-consume in underwater sections; carrying capacity limited by rank

environmental_storytelling.example: Jungle base → warehouse → cargo ship → train → main commander's lair progression mirrors escalation of threat and reveals enemy manufacturing/deployment pipeline
Standard guards vs. officer variants signal faction organization hierarchy; impostor John indicates enemy intelligence capability and deception tactics
Gas-filled rooms establish chemical/WMD threat; underwater sections imply strategic base location; Metal Gear presence indicates mechanical super-weapon
Radar tracking signal appearance indicates enemy surveillance countermeasures; nuke launch countdown reveals existential stakes
Mass-produced Metal Gear tanks on cargo ship show industrialized weapon production; cybernetic Big Boss reveals biotechnical enhancement capability
Rush 'n Attack-style sections represent confined infiltration points (corridors, external cliffs) requiring different tactical approach than top-down stealth

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves in predictable pattern; exclamation mark triggers if player detected; defeated guards may drop ammo or ration
Attacks player; pursues; part of multi-enemy wave during alert phase
Can be interrogated with truth gas to extract intelligence; counts toward promotion alongside hostage rescues
Acts as decoy by allowing himself to be captured; provides tactical support via transceiver
Disguised enemy variant; must be defeated to progress; reveals enemy intelligence operations
Initially hidden inside enemy base; transmits intel via transceiver; exposed as spy during final base infiltration; requires rescue
Commander of hostile nation; boss encounter; reveals survival from original Metal Gear; must be defeated; final threat requiring full combat capability
Scattered in various base locations; rescue triggers promotion when quota met; presence indicates civilian stakes in conflict

notes: Snake's Revenge uses classic NES-era stealth-action visual language. The exclamation mark alert system is its primary danger telegraph—a simple but effective binary (single/double) indicator of threat escalation. Environmental variety (jungle, warehouse, ship, train, base interior) communicates faction capabilities and mission progression. Side-view sections break up top-down stealth with action-platformer mechanics, visually signaling confined or elevated terrain. The game lacks the cardboard box stealth mechanic present in later MGS titles (unused asset in this version), relying instead on guard patrol patterns and alert phase avoidance. Faction identity is communicated through enemy soldier presence, base architecture, and the iconic Metal Gear tank as a super-weapon visual. World state shifts are driven by alert status (three phases: infiltration, single-alert, full-alert) with clear visual markers. The double-agent network (Jennifer, John, Nick) is communicated via transceiver interface rather than environmental presence until key story beats.
